prevalence.source: European allergy epidemiology syntheses; WHO Europe allergy context
prevalence.trend: stable to increasing recognition; food and drug triggers prominent

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; preventable morbidity and rare deaths
burden.mortalityRate: Very low with prompt epinephrine and emergency care

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical criteria (acute onset with airway/breathing/circulation compromise); tryptase supports diagnosis when measured; response to epinephrine

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions with lifetime risk; acute events far fewer
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High where action plans and auto-injectors are available and used correctly

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ED-based diagnosis and specialist allergy follow-up for trigger identification
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Immediate IM epinephrine, airway/oxygen/IV fluids as needed, observation for biphasic reactions, prescribe auto-injector and provide action plan
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Ensuring training and adherence to action plans; variable reimbursement for auto-injectors
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong emergency care access and allergy services; high availability of epinephrine auto-injectors in many countries
